title: Celtics get good news on Jaylen Brown’s Achilles tendinitis injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is cleared to play against the Atlanta Hawks after missing two games with Achilles tendinitis.
Derrick White is probable for the Celtics' game against the Hawks, recovering from a right knee contusion.
Jayson Tatum will not play against Atlanta due to Achilles repair management, following his recent return to action.

title: Major pharmaceutical company to cut nearly 250 Massachusetts jobs
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Takeda will lay off nearly 250 employees at its Cambridge, Massachusetts location as part of a restructuring plan.
The layoffs, starting in July, aim to save $1.25 billion annually by 2028, focusing on operational simplification.
Takeda's restructuring includes moving to a new research hub and follows previous job cuts related to strategic shifts.

title: Lexington to cut a slew of teachers as town prepares to build $660M high school
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lexington Public Schools will cut about 65 full-time positions due to budget constraints and impending $660M high school construction.
Non-renewal letters sent to 160 Lexington educators without professional status, impacting elementary programs like music and art classes.
Lexington faces budget gaps from declining enrollment and rising costs; tax increases for school funds deemed unfavorable by the community.

title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, 22, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mom's dream and raise Alzheimer's awareness.
Wright's family has been deeply affected by Alzheimer's, including her mom diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer's.
She aims to advocate for caregivers, increase research funding, and support families affected by Alzheimer's.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Plymouth Police Officer Samantha Pelrine was suspended by POST after being charged with child rape.
Pelrine and her husband, Daniel Forand, allegedly groomed and sexually abused a boy in their custody from 2018 to 2025.
The couple posted bail and were ordered to surrender firearms and avoid contact with the alleged victim or minors.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police reported seven e-bike incidents in ten days, raising safety concerns and warning of possible tragedy.
A 15-year-old crashed an e-bike into a car on Highlight Street, marking the latest incident; no injuries occurred.
Massachusetts sees rising e-bike incidents, with laws requiring riders to be 16, helmet use; calls for stricter oversight increase.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police alert public about a scam involving a Tom Brady impersonator targeting local families.
A victim was defrauded into sending gift cards to a scammer posing as quarterback Tom Brady.
Authorities advise against sending money to unknown individuals and caution about imposter scams.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda, 52, from Cambridge, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room charity.
The Children's Room supports children and families coping with grief, providing spaces for understanding and healing.
Castaneda views the marathon as a chance to thank Boston for its kindness and contribute to the community.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga's concert at TD Garden featured a dramatic four-act performance built around her new album, Mayhem.
A fan-organized tribute moved Gaga to tears, celebrating her 40th birthday with coordinated signs in the audience.
The show mixed new songs with hits like 'Poker Face' and 'Bad Romance,' engaging the audience with choreographed moments.

title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines to introduce couch-style seating called Relax Row in 2027, offering three-seat configurations for more comfort.
In a reader poll, 61% expressed excitement to try the new seating, while 15% opposed the idea.
Concerns raised include potential noise, privacy issues, and the cost of booking the entire row.
